text: These are extracts from Nikolai A. Voznesenskiy’s book, The Military Economy of the USSR during the Patriotic War. Voznesenskiy was one of the most important Soviet leaders during the war. He played a crucial role in planning the war-time military economy and in organising the development of the East as the main industrial centre of the country, responsible for the uninterrupted provision of arms to the front. He was also in charge of the production of weapons and ammunition for the Red Army, and took part in the Council for the reconstitution of the national economy in the newly-liberated zones.
explanation: The passage is concise and historically themed with specific names and roles; this level of detail could be human-authored or AI-generated, but the neutral, generic phrasing slightly leans toward AI.
